item.title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-09 - Today's Summary
item.description: The AI agent framework category is the clear momentum leader today, driven by two sudden breakout projects. mvanhorn/last30days-skill gained 3,932 stars in 24 hours (acceleration 3.1x), offering a multi-platform search skill that scores results by real engagement—a pattern that suggests developers are hungry for grounded, verifiable agent outputs. rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch continues its sustained hot streak with 9,319 weekly stars, positioning itself as a comprehensive curriculum from math to production agents. Meanwhile, santifer/career-ops (2,630 weekly stars, acceleration 2.1x) shows that AI workflow automation for job search is resonating. The coding agent category remains strong with DeepSeek-Reasonix (4,495 weekly stars), but the spotlight has clearly moved to agent skill composability and educational infrastructure. On Hacker News, three of the top posts discuss AI's impact on careers and progress, reinforcing the theme that developers are grappling with how to build with and adapt to AI agents. The absence of new MCP/tool-calling launches today may indicate a temporary saturation, but the cross-source signal from Apple Core AI and OpenAI's S-1 filing suggests enterprise infrastructure interest persists. Top projects: hpcaitech/ColossalAI, rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch, esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix, santifer/career-ops, mvanhorn/last30days-skill, nexu-io/open-design, affaan-m/ECC, HKUDS/nanobot, addyosmani/agent-skills, sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills, rtk-ai/rtk, getagentseal/codeburn Note: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。 Note: daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。 Note: Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。 Note: Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

item.title: [sudden_breakout] hpcaitech/ColossalAI (+5 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Distributed Training & Inference Infrastructure Description: Colossal-AI is an open-source deep learning system that makes large AI models cheaper, faster, and more accessible through advanced parallelism and heterogeneous memory management. Why it matters: The repo has a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 6 (high), despite a low 7-day average star delta of -0.6. With 41,385 total stars and 4,505 forks, it's a well-established project that recently saw a spike in attention, possibly due to new benchmarks (B200 GPUs showing 50-70% throughput gains) or the release of Open-Sora and DeepSeek fine-tuning guides. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward democratizing large model training: developers can now achieve near-linear scaling on multi-GPU setups with minimal code changes, reducing the barrier to entry for building foundation models. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch (+533 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-ready agents, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact. Why it matters: The repo is on fire: 30,145 stars, +9,045 in the last 7 days (avg 2,075/day), +743 in the last 24 hours, with a high acceleration of 1.5. This is not a flash-in-the-pan spike but a sustained, accelerating wave of attention. The project claims 150k+ readers and 241k page views in the last 30 days, indicating real-world traction beyond GitHub stars. Developer insight: The market is hungry for deep, practical AI education that goes beyond API wrappers. Projects that combine rigorous fundamentals with production-ready outputs are gaining massive traction. This signals a shift from 'learn AI fast' to 'understand AI deeply' as the dominant developer mindset. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix (+702 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent rewritten in Go, optimized for prefix-cache stability and low token costs in long terminal sessions. Why it matters: With 19,939 stars, a 24h star delta of 702, and a 7-day average of 769, the project shows sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio of 1.1 indicates steady growth without sudden spikes, suggesting organic adoption. The recent 1.0 rewrite in Go and active maintenance (commits within 7 days, recent release) keep it relevant. Developer insight: Signals a shift toward model-specific optimizations (prefix caching) and Go-based tooling for AI agents, prioritizing performance and distribution simplicity over ecosystem lock-in. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] santifer/career-ops (+893 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Workflow Automation Description: An AI-powered job search command center that uses Claude Code and other AI coding CLIs to evaluate, tailor, and track job applications at scale. Why it matters: With 50,215 stars, a 24-hour delta of +843, and a 7-day delta of +2,130, this repo is experiencing a sudden breakout (acceleration ratio > 2.0). The velocity is surging: yesterday alone added 458 stars, and the 3-day average is 281. This is not gradual growth—it's a spike, likely driven by recent press coverage (WIRED, Business Insider) and the author's personal case study of landing a Head of AI role using the tool. Developer insight: For AI developers, Career-Ops signals a shift toward agentic, user-empowering tools that automate personal workflows. The integration with multiple AI CLIs (Claude, Gemini, etc.) shows the value of building CLI-first, model-agnostic systems. It also highlights the growing trend of 'AI for the job seeker' as a counterbalance to AI-powered applicant tracking systems. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] mvanhorn/last30days-skill (+3932 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: An AI agent skill that searches Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web in parallel, scoring results by real engagement (upvotes, likes, money) to produce a grounded, synthesized brief on any topic. Why it matters: The repo exploded with 1,511 stars in the last 24 hours (538 yesterday, 504 three-day average) and an acceleration ratio of 3, indicating a sudden breakout. With 29,788 total stars and 2,512 forks, it's clearly hitting a nerve. The v3 release (intelligent search, shareable HTML briefs, cross-source clustering) likely triggered the surge, combined with strong community adoption via Claude Code and other agent hosts. Developer insight: The success of /last30days signals a shift toward agent-driven, multi-platform research tools that bypass traditional search. For AI developers, the opportunity is in building similar 'bridge' agents that connect walled gardens (social APIs, prediction markets, niche forums) and rank by real-world signals. The v3 intelligent pre-research pattern—resolving entities before searching—is a key architectural insight. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] nexu-io/open-design (+884 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: Open-source, local-first desktop app that turns coding agents into design engines — a Claude Design alternative with 259+ skills, 142+ design systems, and MCP integration for 21+ CLIs. Why it matters: With 61,833 stars, a 24h delta of +884, a 7d delta of +5,251, and an acceleration of 0.9 (high), this project is sustaining explosive growth. The recent 0.9.0 release with a built-in Model Router (no setup) and the Open Design Fellow program signal a push to convert hype into community-driven adoption. The combination of local-first, agent-native design generation taps into the current 'vibe coding' and AI-assisted prototyping zeitgeist. Developer insight: Open Design signals a shift from 'AI generates code' to 'AI generates design artifacts directly.' For AI developers, this means the next frontier is not just code completion but full artifact generation (UI, docs, presentations) using the same agent infrastructure. The BYOK proxy and MCP-first architecture are patterns worth adopting. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] affaan-m/ECC (+1174 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: A harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance across multiple coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) with skills, memory, security scanning, and continuous learning. Why it matters: With 211K+ stars, 32K+ forks, and a sustained 7-day average of 1,757 stars/day (acceleration 0.8), this project is a massive, sustained phenomenon. The 24h delta of 1,174 stars and recent v2.0.0-rc.1 release (Apr 2026) with a Rust control-plane alpha, dashboard GUI, and 64 agents/261 skills show active development and community momentum. It's the #1 trending repo by star velocity in the AI agent tooling space. Developer insight: This signals a shift from single-agent configs to multi-harness operator systems. The community is voting with stars that a unified performance layer across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc. is critical. The Rust alpha (ECC 2.0) suggests the project is evolving into a more robust, low-level control plane—worth watching for AI tooling infrastructure trends. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] HKUDS/nanobot (+76 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A lightweight, open-source personal AI agent that you can truly own, with WebUI, chat channels, tools, memory, MCP, and automation. Why it matters: With 43,906 stars, a 7-day average of 71.7 stars/day, and a 24-hour delta of 76, this project shows sustained high attention. The acceleration ratio of 1 indicates steady growth without sudden spikes, but the consistent daily star gain and recent v0.2.1 release (June 1) keep it in the spotlight. Developer insight: The trend toward self-hosted, multi-provider AI agents is accelerating. Nanobot's modular design and active development signal a shift away from vendor lock-in toward composable, open-source AI workflows. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] addyosmani/agent-skills (+402 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A curated pack of 23 production-grade engineering skills packaged as Markdown workflows for AI coding agents. Why it matters: With 49,354 stars and a sustained 7-day average of 292 stars/day, this project is experiencing consistent, high-velocity growth. The 24-hour delta of 402 stars and acceleration of 1.3 indicate strong, ongoing interest without a sudden spike. It's clearly resonating with the developer community as a practical tool for improving AI agent output quality. Developer insight: The success of this repo signals a shift in how developers interact with AI coding tools: from treating them as autocomplete-on-steroids to delegating structured workflows. The demand for 'agent skills' suggests the next frontier is not better models but better orchestration of model capabilities. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills (+123 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: A massive, installable library of 1,525+ reusable agentic skills (SKILL.md playbooks) for AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Antigravity. Why it matters: With 40,089 stars, a 7-day average delta of 100.1 stars/day, and an acceleration of 1.8 (high but not sudden breakout), this project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity organic growth. The 24h delta of 175 stars and 3-day average of 96.7 confirm strong ongoing interest. It's not a flash-in-the-pan spike but a steadily accelerating adoption curve, likely driven by the expanding ecosystem of AI coding tools and the need for reusable skill libraries. Developer insight: The rise of skill libraries signals a shift from ad-hoc prompting to structured, reusable agent instructions. This project is a leading indicator that the AI coding assistant ecosystem is maturing, with developers demanding composable, tool-agnostic skill packages rather than isolated prompt snippets. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] rtk-ai/rtk (+454 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies Why it matters: MCP / Tool Calling 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +454，总 stars 60,229，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。 Developer insight: 重点看协议适配、工具注册和真实示例，判断它是否能降低 agent 接工具的成本。 Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] getagentseal/codeburn (+52 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: See where your AI coding tokens go. Interactive TUI dashboard for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor cost observability. Why it matters: AI Agent Framework 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +52，总 stars 7,771，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。 Developer insight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。 Risk: Low
